notes: Deep clinical pastoral experience in oncology. Compassionate presence, strong theology of suffering. Excellent fit for Bluff City Medical Center culture.
Solid CPE fundamentals; good reflective practice. Could use more critical care exposure but coachable. Recommend for Cohort A slot.
Exceptional CPE supervision track record from Vanderbilt. Would strengthen our CPE program significantly. Enthusiastic endorsement.
Warm pastoral demeanor. Has navigated complicated grief situations with families. Would benefit from trauma-informed care training before start date.
Volunteer coordination experience was administrative, not chaplaincy-grounded. Lacks theological depth needed for this role at the director level.
Strong listen-first approach; patient families would feel held by her. Her oncology experience at Regional One Health directly matches what we need. Recommend offer.
Experienced in live AM production workflow. Familiar with faith-based formatting. Good energy for the Morning Reflections brand. Recommend second conversation with Rebecca.
Background in counseling brings relational skills. Needs orientation on hospital spiritual care context but motivated. Good candidate for Volunteer Coordinator.
Church Health Center experience is outstanding preparation. Community connections in Memphis will help recruit volunteers. Highly recommended for Volunteer Coordinator offer.
On-call chaplaincy experience is solid. Flexible scheduling; comfortable with overnight rotation. Would round out our ICU float coverage well.
Bereavement specialization from hospice setting is exactly what the part-time role requires. Board certification current. Ready to extend offer.

Crescent City chaplaincy training is solid academically. Less clinical hours than ideal for ICU/ED pace. Would benefit from additional supervised shifts before independent assignment. Moving forward cautiously.
